channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Things to know about the ruling blocking President Trump's refugee ban
item.description: President Donald Trump’s effort to suspend the system for resettling refugees in the U.S. is on hold after a federal judge in Seattle blocked it.
item.dc:creator: Gene Johnson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The US lines up Latin American cooperation for migrant deportations
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Venezuelan migrants handed over to Mexico like it’s a U.S. immigration detention facility. Families from Central Asia flown to Panama and Costa Rica to await voluntary repatriation to their countries.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Teachers union sues over Trump administration's deadline to end school diversity programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new federal lawsuit in Maryland is challenging a Trump administration memo giving the nation’s schools and universities two weeks to eliminate “race-based” practices of any kind or risk losing their federal money.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge OKs prison abuse settlement, rejecting Trump administration's push to rewrite protections
item.description: A judge has given final approval to a legal settlement that subjects the federal Bureau of Prisons to independent monitoring and increased transparency in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse at a now-shuttered federal women’s prison in California.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US and Ukraine near an economic deal with mineral rights but no security promise, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals, three senior Ukrainian officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, HANNA ARHIROVA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: US energy secretary touts nuclear power as tech sector's thirst for electricity grows
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright says it’s critical that the nation be out in front when it comes to artificial intelligence, and that means having reliable and affordable sources of electricity to meet the growing demands of the technology sector.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: House GOP pushes 'big' budget resolution to passage, a crucial step toward delivering Trump's agenda
item.description: With a push from President Donald Trump, House Republicans sent a GOP budget blueprint to passage Tuesday, a step toward delivering his “big, beautiful bill” with $4.5 trillion in tax breaks and $2 trillion in spending cuts despite a wall of opposition from Democrats and discomfort among Republicans
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Young American tourists found dead at Caribbean beach resort; authorities investigating
item.description: Belize officials announced Monday that three American tourists were recently found dead at a beach resort in San Pedro. The women were between 23 and 26.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Adnan Syed's murder conviction still stands as he seeks sentence reduction in 'Serial' case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Despite documented problems with the evidence against him and an earlier request from prosecutors to clear his record, Adnan Syed will remain a convicted murderer, according to court papers filed Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House GOP adopts Trump budget after topsy-turvy night
item.description: House Republicans adopted the budget resolution that will lay the foundation for enacting President Trump's legislative agenda Tuesday night, just minutes after they initially pulled the measure from the floor.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family of fallen Virginia police officer say expecting father, 25, 'never hesitated' to help those in need
item.description: Family members and peers remember a fallen Virginia Beach police officer, who was expecting his first child in the coming months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Southwest Airlines flight abruptly rises to avoid another plane crossing Chicago runway
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Pilots on a Southwest Airlines flight attempting to land at Chicago's Midway Airport were forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and KATHLEEN FOODY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Another $500M for Hurricane Helene relief in North Carolina passes key hurdle
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Another $500 million would be spent toward Hurricane Helene recovery efforts in western North Carolina in legislation the state House unanimously approved Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missouri woman accused in plot to sell Graceland says she'll plead guilty
item.description: A Missouri woman said Tuesday she is pleading guilty to a federal charge accusing her of concocting a brazen plot to defraud Elvis Presley’s family by trying to auction off his Graceland mansion and property before a judge halted the mysterious foreclosure sale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New search to begin for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370: Reports
item.description: The latest search comes after the Malaysian government in December agreed to accept a second "no find, no fee" proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Dragons to fly over Universal’s ‘How To Train Your Dragon’ land
item.description: Walking and flying dragons will greet guests as they explore How to Train Your Dragon – Isle of Berk, one of the five immersive lands that will be featured in the all-new Universal Epic Universe set to open on May 22. The theme park resort teased the news in a blog post released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: A federal judge in Seattle blocks Trump’s effort to halt the refugee admissions system
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge in Seattle on Tuesday blocked President Donald Trump’s suspension of the nation’s refugee admissions system, saying that while the president has broad authority over who comes into the country, he cannot nullify the law passed by Congress establishing the program.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Idaho prosecutors seek to limit Bryan Kohberger's alibi evidence at murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Idaho prosecutors say a man charged in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students shouldn't be allowed to offer an alibi defense unless he takes the stand himself during the murder trial later this year.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Scenes at US Capitol on war's anniversary reflect partisan divide on Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 100 House members at one point packed the steps of the U.S. Capitol to show their support for Ukraine following Russia's invasion three years ago, Republicans joining with Democrats in a forceful display of bipartisanship.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2025 Black History Month theme recognizes labor activists
item.description: The Association for the Study of African American Life and History’s 2025 Black History Month theme is “African Americans and Labor.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: 21 DOGE staffers resign over Musk's demands
item.description: In a resignation letter, the tech workers said they wouldn't help "dismantle critical public services," as Elon Musk and DOGE try to cut the size of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, KTLA 5

item.title: Princeton man shared eerie poems on social media before allegedly killing former star athlete brother, cat
item.description: The Princeton, New Jersey, man accused of killing his former college athlete brother and a cat posted eerie messages to social media months ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky expected to visit White House amid push for minerals deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to visit the White House on Friday as Kyiv and the Trump administration work to finalize an agreement on critical minerals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Don't expect a Supplemental Security Income check next month: Here's why
item.description: Those who regularly receive Supplemental Security Income from the Social Security Administration have seen some irregularly-timed payments recently. In March, they won't see a payment at all. Supplemental Security Income (SSI) is dispersed monthly, typically on the first day of each month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Man forced to sit next to corpse after woman died midway through flight
item.description: Passenger Mitchell Ring said the flight crew did everything they could to revive the woman but they were unsuccessful.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Video: Fishermen spot pair of whales off Florida coast
item.description: The Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission said it is rare for right whales to be spotted in the Gulf area.
item.dc:creator: Leah Burdick, NewsNation

item.title: Woman injured on Harry Potter theme park ride awarded $7.25 million
item.description: A federal jury has awarded $7.25 million to a 74-year-old Arizona woman for a spine injury she suffered in a fall while exiting the Harry Potter and the Forbidden Journey ride at Universal Studios Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Proposed Idaho state law could make some lies a felony crime with prison time
item.description: An Idaho lawmaker is pushing a bill that, if passed, would make it a felony to knowingly lie and communicate a false statement about another person.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Orange Crush 2025: Georgia beach town in permit showdown over crime-fueled college fest
item.description: Tybee Island Mayor Brian West explains how the city plans to curb crime, traffic issues, litter and other problems during the Orange Crush spring break event in April.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mother of Palestinian American boy slain in suburban Chicago hate crime testifies at trial
item.description: Prosecutors say a suburban Chicago landlord took a knife from a belt holder and attacked a Palestinian American woman before fatally stabbing her young son 26 times in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Coast Guard rescues 3 boaters stuck at sea for 9 hours after their boat capsizes
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Coast Guard crews rescued three boaters in Captiva Island, Florida, after their boat flipped over and they were stuck at sea for nine hours. A family member reported concerns at 1 a.m. Sunday after not hearing from the three boaters since 8 p.m. Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge blocks Department of Education, OPM from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday temporarily barred the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing troves of sensitive personal data from federal agencies. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Mystics trade 2-time All-Star Ariel Atkins to the Sky for No. 3 pick in WNBA draft
item.description: The Washington Mystics accelerated their rebuild, trading two-time All-Star Ariel Atkins to the Chicago Sky for the No. 3 overall pick in this year's draft on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Briscoe tries to move past devastating penalties and Blaney to start on pole in NASCAR Atlanta race
item.description: A massive fine left Chase Briscoe facing a huge points deficit heading into Sunday's NASCAR Cup Series race at Atlanta Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: Charles Odum, Fox 5
